methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Deterrence is a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by Dhruva Rajendra and Henry Olgers, having raised $10M+ to date. Valuation analysis is anchored to a comparable universe of 10 peers spanning 2 public companies and 8 private firms operating in the same sector, including Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, and Saronic.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement to the $10M raised proxy, implying a $25M valuation. Peer-median analysis draws from four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables: Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed). The peer set's median of $225M reflects the wide dispersion across the group, with valuations ranging from $60M to $500M.

The two methods produce a triangulated range of $21M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $125M. The wide band reflects meaningful uncertainty around Deterrence's competitive positioning within a fragmented peer cohort. The low-end estimate anchors to stage-progression mechanics, while the high-end incorporates the upper quartile of early-stage defense and security comps. Key uncertainties include customer concentration, revenue traction relative to peers, and the pace of customer acquisition in government and enterprise segments.
